Streamlining Procurement Through Digital Transformation: Enhancing
Efficiency and Compliance
A. S. Al-Farsi and I. Al Mamari, OQ Exploration & Production LLC

Abstract
OQ Exploration and Production (OQEP) is a leading player in Oman's oil and gas sector, playing a key role
in maximizing the value of the nation's energy resources. As part of the OQ Group, OQEP supports Oman’s
economic growth by optimizing exploration and production efforts. The company’s operations are integral
to advancing Oman Vision 2040. Currently, OQEP’s procurement evaluation process for in-house items
relies on multiple manuals, paper-based stages, particularly in the technical bid evaluation. This process is
time-consuming, involving extensive data collection and review, leading to delays, technical queries, and
potential loss of information. These inefficiencies result in significant time and effort spent on evaluations,
with a risk of missing crucial details. To address these challenges, OQEP aims to transition to a more
streamlined, digital system. The proposed Artificial Intelligence based system will automate data extraction
from bid submissions, evaluate compliance with minimum requirements, and generate reports, significantly
improving accuracy and efficiency. It will serve as an open platform for real-time project tracking and
issue resolution, accessible to relevant stakeholders. Additionally, the system will act as a comprehensive
database for all procurement activities, consolidating historical data for easy management and access. This
digital transformation is expected to reduce evaluation time, improve communication between the company
and suppliers, and ultimately enhance procurement efficiency, making the process faster, more organized,
and more effective.
Introduction
OQ Exploration and Production (OQEP) is a prominent subsidiary of the OQ Group, which plays a vital
role in Oman’s oil and gas industry. As a leading entity in the exploration and production sector, OQEP is
dedicated to maximizing the value of the country’s energy resources, contributing significantly to Oman’s
economic development. The company is engaged in the full spectrum of upstream activities, including
the exploration, drilling, and production of oil and gas. OQEP’s operations are integral to the broader
strategic goals outlined in Oman Vision 2040, which aims to diversify the nation’s economy and increase
the value of its energy exports. By utilizing advanced technologies and optimizing its operations, OQEP
helps improve the efficiency and sustainability of Oman’s energy resources. The company is also committed
to environmental responsibility and has implemented several initiatives to reduce its ecological footprint.

With its strong focus on innovation and operational excellence, OQEP plays a critical role in shaping the
future of Oman’s energy sector while supporting the nation’s long-term growth and development.
A critical aspect of OQEP’s projects involves its procurement evaluation process, which encompasses
the sourcing and evaluation of items and services required for its energy projects. Despite the importance
of procurement, the current system is largely manual and paper-based, making it prone to inefficiencies,
delays, and human errors. The technical bid evaluation process, in particular, is labor-intensive, requiring
the review of detailed submissions, data collection, and compliance verification.
The time and effort spent on manual method to perform the technical bid evaluation in the oil and gas
industry significantly hamper efficiency, productivity, and employee satisfaction. With manual processes
contributing to 60%-70% longer evaluation times, increased risk of human errors which lead to cost
implications. The technical evaluation process challenges are summarized as follows:
• Time Consumption in Manual Evaluation
I.
Average Time Spent on Bid Template Preparation:
The preparation of technical bid evaluation templates is a time-intensive process,
requiring approximately 5 to 15 hours depending on the complexity of items to
be evaluated. This process involves gathering detailed specifications, organizing data
into a structured format, and ensuring consistency and accuracy across all templates.
Additionally, coordination between multiple departments and stakeholders for input further
extends the preparation time.
II.
Average Time Spent on Bid Evaluation:
Manual bid evaluation can take up to 60%-70% longer than expected automated
methods. Reviewing each bid manually can take anywhere from 8 to 10 hours per bid.
Paper-based processes are significantly slower.
III.
Time Wasted on Compliance Checks:
The manual data entry, evaluation, and verification can add 30%-40% more time to the
technical evaluation. Since oil and gas bids typically involve a large volume of technical
data, verifying compliance with technical requirements manually can consume substantial
hours.
IV.
Average time spent on Technical Queries preparation:
The manual preparation of technical queries significantly extends the evaluation process,
adding an additional 10%-15% to the overall timeline. This includes drafting the queries,
communicating them to vendors, and subsequently receiving, reviewing, and analyzing
vendor responses. The lack of automation increases the time and effort required, often
leading to inefficiencies and delays.
V.
Average time Spent to complete the Evaluation:
Based on the processes outlined in points I through IV, the company typically
experiences a delay of 4-5 weeks in completing the bid evaluation process. This timeframe
excludes additional delays caused by vendor response times to technical queries, which
further prolongs the overall timeline and impacts project schedules.
• Employee Effort and Efficiency
I.
High Employee Workload:
Technical team in the oil and gas industry can spend 70%-80% of their time on manual
data entry, review, and bid evaluation, which significantly detracts from their ability to
focus on other assigned tasks. In complex item procurements, the number of employees
involved in manual bid evaluations can be extensive, typically, include 4-5 employees per
item procurement.

3
II.
Manual Errors and Rework:
Manual evaluations often lead to errors in data entry or compliance checks, which must
be corrected through rework. The time spent on rework can double the effort required for
initial evaluations.
III.
Risk of Missing Critical Information:
Through the manual method, there is an increased risk of missing critical data in bid
submissions, which can lead to delays. Manual processes in procurement evaluation can
cause up to a 20%-25% loss of critical information across multiple stages of evaluation.
• Unpredicted Cost
Inefficiencies, delays, and human errors in manual procurement evaluation processes can result
unpredicted increase in procurement costs, primarily due to design changes after the award stage,
which lead to cost implications.
• Inefficiencies in Communication
Manual processes involve continuous back-and-forth communication between technical team,
project team, contract team, suppliers, and OEM, leading to delays from slow responses to queries.
Additionally, the transfer of queries and special requests to bidders can be missed due to the manual
communication through emails.
These challenges highlight the need for a more efficient and modern approach to perform the technical
evaluation process, leading OQEP to seek a digital solution to address these challenges.
This paper presents the rationale for digitalizing OQEP’s procurement processes, with a focus on the
automation of technical bid evaluations. It explores the current challenges, the proposed digitalization (e.g.
AI-based solution), and the expected improvements in efficiency, accuracy, and communication throughout
the procurement lifecycle.
Statement of Theory and Definitions
The primary theory underlying the digital transformation at OQEP is the concept of Automation and
Artificial Intelligence applied to business processes. AI refers to the use of machine learning algorithms
to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as data analysis and decision-making.
In procurement evaluation process, AI can be used to automate tasks like data extraction, data entry,
compliance checks, technical queries preparation and final report generation, improving speed and accuracy.
Key terms in this paper include:
• Technical Bid Evaluation: The process of assessing bids from suppliers based on predefined
criteria, technical data and specifications.
• Artificial Intelligence (AI): Machine learning algorithms used to automate processes such as data
extraction and compliance evaluation.
• Automation: The use of technology to perform tasks that traditionally require human intervention.
Description and Application of Digitalize the Process
The AI-based digital system proposed by OQEP is designed to revolutionize the company's procurement
evaluation process by automating the data extraction and compliance evaluation stages, which will
significantly enhance operational efficiency, accuracy, and speed. This digitalized system uses advanced
machine learning algorithms that are tailored to the specific needs of the oil and gas industry, optimizing
the procurement evaluation lifecycle.
System Overview.   The digitalized system will enable smooth data flow between the various stages of
the procurement evaluation process. It begins by automatically extracting predefined data from technical
Downloaded from http://onepetro.org/SPEGOTS/proceedings-pdf/25GOTS/25GOTS/D021S027R001/4728754/spe-224715-ms.pdf/1 by Schlumberger Oilfield UK Plc user on 27 September 2025

bid submissions, including technical specifications, vendor names and key compliance documents. The
system is designed to verify that each bidder meets the minimum compliance requirements. These
requirements typically include documents such as a Table of Compliance for technical specifications,
Quality Requirements, Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) standards, and In-Country Value (ICV)
details. By automating the extraction, entering and verification of these key pieces of information, the system
reduces the likelihood of human error and significantly speeds up the process.
Stage 1: Go-No-Go Stage (Initial Compliance Filtering).   In the initial stage of the technical evaluation,
referred to as the "Go-No-Go" stage, the system filters bidders based on a predefined set of compliance
criteria. The system evaluates whether each bidder has submitted all necessary documents and whether the
data provided meets the basic requirements for the project. This stage serves as a gatekeeping mechanism,
ensuring that only bidders who comply with the company initial bids submissions documentation move
forward in the technical evaluation process.
The system's algorithms cross-check the submitted documents against the compliance criteria, flagging
any missing or incomplete documents, such as Signed and stamped Table of Compliance for technical
specifications, Reference Experience List for supplying the requested material, HSE requirements, ICV plan
and quality certifications. For example, if a bidder fails to meet specific HSE requirements or does not submit
an adequate ICV plan, the system will automatically reject their bid and exclude them from the subsequent
evaluation stages. This reduces the time spent by technical teams manually reviewing submissions and helps
prevent unnecessary delays.
At the conclusion of this stage, the system generates a list of qualified bidders who have met all the initial
requirements and are eligible to move on to the next phase of evaluation. This list is essential for focusing
the resources of the technical evaluation team on the most qualified bidders.
Stage 2: Detailed Technical Evaluation.   Once the qualified bidders are identified, the system moves
into the second stage, which is the detailed technical evaluation. At this point, the system compares the
detailed technical offers submitted by the bidders against a set of predetermined, project-specific technical
requirements. These requirements typically include detailed specifications, such as design parameters,
equipment standards, performance metrics, and adherence to industry best practices.
Using machine learning, the system can quickly identify compliance or deviations from these technical
requirements. It generates detailed compliance reports that show where each bidder’s submission aligns
or diverges from the specified technical criteria. For instance, if a bidder’s technical submission differs
in terms of material quality, testing requirements, or design specifications, the system will highlight these
discrepancies and provide a clear comparison for each bidder’s proposal. This transparency enables the
evaluation team to easily assess how well each bidder meets the required technical specifications.
Additionally, the system allows users to generate technical queries for each bidder. For example, if a
bidder’s proposal contains discrepancies, the system will create a technical queries report that can be used
to send to the bidder for clarification or further explanation, these queries can be modified, detailed and
evaluated by the focal technical person. This enables the technical team to have organized list of technical
queries, ensuring that all queries are tracked, responded to, and resolved. This process eliminates the need
of manual listing of technical queries and reduces the risk of missing critical information.
After all queries are addressed and the necessary clarifications are made, the system generates a finalized
technical evaluation report and issue it to be electronically singed by all evaluation stakeholders. This
report includes a list of disqualified bidders and technically qualified bidders who meet all the necessary
specifications and are eligible to proceed to the commercial evaluation phase.
Collaboration and Real-Time Input from the Technical Team.   One of the key benefits of the proposed AI-
based system is the ability to facilitate collaboration among multiple stakeholders involved in the technical
evaluation process. The system will allow all members of the technical evaluation team to access the
Downloaded from http://onepetro.org/SPEGOTS/proceedings-pdf/25GOTS/25GOTS/D021S027R001/4728754/spe-224715-ms.pdf/1 by Schlumberger Oilfield UK Plc user on 27 September 2025

5
same evaluation document in real time, ensuring that each team member can simultaneously input their
observations, evaluations, and technical queries into the system.
This collaborative feature promotes greater transparency and coordination among the evaluation team.
Team members can view each other's inputs, add their own comments, and raise concerns directly within
the shared evaluation sheet. By enabling real-time updates, the system minimizes the risk of mismatched
information or discrepancies between team members’ assessments. This feature also ensures that everyone
involved in the evaluation is on the same page, which is crucial when dealing with complex technical
specifications and large amounts of data.
Furthermore, the system’s collaborative approach ensures that any discrepancies or deviations from the
specifications are flagged and addressed by the relevant experts. For example, if an issue arises regarding
the interpretation of a particular requirement, the system allows technical experts from different disciplines
to communicate and come to an agreement, thus reducing potential misunderstandings and enhancing the
overall quality of the evaluation.
Management follow-up and Timeline Tracking.   The system also offers significant advantages for
management follow-up and project tracking. The digital platform provides a comprehensive overview of
the procurement process, showing the current status and the stage of completion for each bid evaluation.
A timeline is displayed, outlining key milestones and deadlines for each stage of the evaluation process.
This transparency enables managers to easily monitor the progress of the technical evaluation process and
ensure that it stays on track.
Moreover, the system includes a built-in review cycle for technical queries, ensuring that each query
undergoes a set period of review before being closed. The review cycle timing is clearly displayed, allowing
management to track how long each query takes to resolve and whether the technical evaluation process
is being completed within the expected timeframe. This feature provides an added layer of accountability,
helping to prevent delays and ensuring that the technical evaluation process meets deadlines.
Centralized Database.   The AI-based digital system will function as a centralized database for all
procurement activities at OQEP, ensuring that every aspect of the technical evaluation process is thoroughly
documented and easily accessible. This database will consolidate all technical evaluation data, including
bid submissions, compliance reports, technical evaluations, communications, and historical records, into a
single, organized platform.
By centralizing this information, the system will provide stakeholders with an access to critical
data, improving transparency and decision-making. The system will store comprehensive records of all
technical evaluation process, which can be referenced for future projects, audits, or performance reviews.
Additionally, this centralized approach will streamline data management, allowing for quick retrieval of
information, reducing the risk of lost or misplaced documents, and ensuring that all procurement activities
are accurately tracked and maintained for long-term operational efficiency.
Presentation of Data and Results
The primary data generated from the implementation of the AI-based system will focus on key performance
indicators, such as the speed and accuracy of technical bid evaluations, when compared to the current manual
process. The expected results include the following:
• Reduction in Evaluation Time: One of the most significant improvements expected from the AI
system is the reduction in the time spent on bid evaluations. Currently, manual evaluations require
extensive effort to extract data, cross-check compliance, and review each submission individually.
With AI-powered automation, data extraction and compliance checking can be completed in a
fraction of the time. The system will automatically analyze and categorize bid data, flagging any
discrepancies, which enables technical evaluation teams to focus on making informed decisions
Downloaded from http://onepetro.org/SPEGOTS/proceedings-pdf/25GOTS/25GOTS/D021S027R001/4728754/spe-224715-ms.pdf/1 by Schlumberger Oilfield UK Plc user on 27 September 2025

SPE-224715-MS
rather than spending valuable time on administrative tasks. As a result, completing the evaluation
will be faster, and procurement cycles will shorten, allowing OQEP to handle more procured items
in less time, driving overall efficiency.
• Improved Accuracy: The system is designed to minimize human error by automating the data
entry and compliance verification processes. In the current manual process, there is a risk of
overlooking key details or making mistakes while transcribing and evaluating complex bid
information. By removing these manual steps, the AI system ensures greater consistency and
reliability in compliance evaluations, leading to more accurate results. This will help avoid costly
errors that may arise from misunderstandings or missing important details, ensuring that only
compliant bids proceed to the next stages of the technical evaluation process.
• Enhanced Communication: Effective communication is critical to a successful technical
evaluation process, but manual systems often lead to delays and misunderstandings due to the
reliance on emails, phone calls, and fragmented communication channels. The AI system, however,
provides a centralized digital platform for real-time tracking. Project managers, procurement teams,
and other stakeholders will have access to up-to-date information on the status of bid evaluations,
including technical queries, clarifications, and updates from bidders. This transparency will
significantly reduce delays caused by miscommunication, ensuring that all parties are aligned and
informed throughout the process. Additionally, the platform will support instant notifications and
reminders, ensuring that technical teams respond quickly to any issues or queries, further reducing
response time.
• Document and Report Generation: The system will automate the creation of essential
procurement documents, including technical evaluation reports, compliance summaries, and other
related documentation. In the manual process, generating these reports often involves extensive
copying, pasting, and compiling of data from multiple sources, which can be time-consuming and
prone to inconsistencies. The AI system will streamline this process by automatically compiling
and formatting reports based on the data entered into the system, ensuring consistency across all
documentation. This automated document generation will not only save significant time and effort
but will also improve the overall professionalism and accuracy of the reports. Furthermore, these
automated reports will be stored in the centralized database, allowing for easy access and retrieval
for audits, historical reference, or future procurement projects.
• Pilot phase
The AI based solution shall be tested through a pilot phase before full implementation to ensure
the functionality and accuracy of the system to avoid systematic errors which can lead to fail the
objective of the digitized solution. The Test Procedures for the AI-based solution will include all
the defined stages and expected results.
By addressing these areas of improvement—evaluation time, accuracy, communication, and
documentation—the AI-based system will lead to a more efficient, effective, and organized procurement
process at OQEP, ultimately contributing to better decision-making, cost savings, and smoother project
execution.
Conclusions
The digital transformation of procurement at OQEP, driven by an Artificial Intelligence system, represents
a significant step toward improving efficiency, accuracy, and communication in the Company’s projects.
By automating technical bid evaluations, the Company can reduce manual effort, accelerate procurement
cycles, and enhance the decision-making process. The results of this transformation are expected to lead
to a more efficient procurement process, improving collaboration among stakeholders and ensuring that
critical information is not lost between stages. This move toward digitalization is aligned with OQEP’s
Downloaded from http://onepetro.org/SPEGOTS/proceedings-pdf/25GOTS/25GOTS/D021S027R001/4728754/spe-224715-ms.pdf/1 by Schlumberger Oilfield UK Plc user on 27 September 2025

goals of operational excellence and resource optimization, ultimately contributing to the company’s long-
term competitiveness and supporting Oman’s Vision 2040.
The successful implementation of this AI-driven system will demonstrate how technology can be
leveraged to streamline complex procurement processes and enhance overall business operations. Further
research and testing will be necessary to refine the system, but the early results indicate that this digital
solution has the potential to deliver substantial improvements to OQEP’s technical evaluation process and
beyond.
This paper provides an in-depth exploration of the digitalization of procurement at OQEP, emphasizing
the role of AI in transforming traditional workflows into more efficient, data-driven processes. The results
of this transformation will not only benefit OQEP but could also have significant implications for other
organizations in the oil and gas sector looking to modernize their procurement practices.
